methodsAttempted.query: site:ou.edu University of Oklahoma artificial intelligence policy students faculty generative AI

methodsAttempted.query: site:ou.edu academic integrity artificial intelligence generative AI University of Oklahoma
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found OU Libraries academic integrity guidance and an OU Academic Integrity Programs PDF candidate.

methodsAttempted.query: OU PolicyStat AI policy quick links
methodsAttempted.notes: OU public policy hub linked five PolicyStat AI policy pages; Firecrawl could not access the content without OU SSO or failed to load the page.

artifacts.summary: Discovery used Firecrawl search and scrape first. Public OU and OU Libraries pages provided usable official evidence; OU PolicyStat AI policies were official candidates but login-gated or failed to load, so they were recorded as inaccessible and not used as claim evidence.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Oklahoma maintains a public AI policy hub that points users to AI guidance for students, staff, governance, teaching, and research, while describing OU AI policies as intended to support responsible, ethical, and transparent AI use.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: OU teaching guidance says the university does not mandate a single classroom approach to AI; faculty are encouraged to make intentional, discipline-appropriate choices and communicate them clearly to students.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: OU identifies Microsoft Copilot with organizational data protection as available for faculty and staff and describes it as the recommended way to use generative AI within the OU environment.

artifacts.claimText: OU Libraries academic integrity guidance says AI tool use should be disclosed and cited according to the relevant discipline style guide, and it recommends documenting AI-assisted work and verifying AI-generated content before use.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: OU's AI policies are intended to support responsible, ethical, and transparent use of AI across teaching, research, and operations. Faculty are encouraged to familiarize themselves with these policies, particularly when incorporating AI into coursework or research workflows.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI raises important questions about learning goals, assessment, authorship, and academic integrity. OU does not mandate a single approach to AI in the classroom. Instead, faculty are encouraged to make intentional, discipline-appropriate choices and to communicate those choices clearly to students.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Microsoft Copilot with organizational data protection (formerly Bing Chat Enterprise) is an everyday AI companion; providing Chat GPT4 AI-powered chat for the web. Copilot is available for use by OU faculty and staff. It is the recommended way to use generative AI within the OU environment.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Citation Guidelines: when using AI tools, always disclose and cite your usage following your discipline's style guide. artifacts.notes: Approved as a source-status candidate about a public OU AI policy hub; it does not assert that inaccessible PolicyStat pages were verified.
Approved as a narrow teaching-guidance claim based on the public OU CFE page.
Approved as a tool-treatment claim scoped to OU faculty and staff availability and OU environment recommendation.
Approved as a privacy/security-adjacent claim because OU IT directly states the sensitive-data limitation.
Approved as a library academic-integrity guidance claim; it is not framed as a binding university-wide rule.

artifacts.title: University of Oklahoma AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl for QS 2026 row 564 found public OU AI guidance pages covering a policy hub, classroom approach guidance, recommended Copilot use for faculty and staff, sensitive-data limits for public AI tools, and library academic-integrity guidance. Official PolicyStat AI policies were discovered but not used as evidence because Firecrawl either reached OU SSO or the PolicyStat host was robots-disallowed.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
PolicyStat AI policy content was not crawled because public attempts were blocked by robots or redirected to OU SSO.
Claims are staged OpenClaw candidates only and are not published canonical records.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
